title: Write-Off and Recovery
event: Ortiz Co. writes off a $1,500 account deemed uncollectible. Two months later, the customer unexpectedly pays in full. Prepare all entries.

lines.label: cushion used
lines.why: The write-off consumes allowance that was expensed in advance.
lines.trap: NO Bad Debt Expense here — the expense already happened when the estimate was recorded.

lines.label: specific account removed
lines.why: The specific customer's balance comes off the books.
lines.trap: Net realizable value is UNCHANGED — both AR and the allowance drop by 1,500.

lines.label: claim revived
lines.why: Put the account back — it turned out to be collectible after all.
lines.trap: Reinstate BEFORE recording the cash; the customer's credit history should show the recovery.

lines.label: cushion restored
lines.trap: Not revenue, not a gain — just the estimate un-consuming itself.

lines.label: claim settled
lines.trap: Total income effect across all three entries: zero. Everything ran through balance-sheet accounts.

memorize.body: Under the allowance method, neither write-offs nor recoveries touch income — the expense lives entirely in the year-end estimate.
Write-offs leave net realizable value unchanged: AR and the allowance fall together.

questions.prompt: Bad debt EXPENSE recorded at the moment of the $1,500 write-off = ?

distractors.feedback: The expense was estimated in advance — the write-off just spends allowance that already exists.

build.scaffold: Write-off: allowance vs AR. Recovery: reverse it, then collect normally. Income never moves.
